test_cases.test_case: Successful login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard

steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: redirects to Dashboard

preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <invalid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating invalid credentials; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered value

test_cases.test_case: Submit with ALL required fields empty

3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on the Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; user remains on the login page (no redirect to Dashboard)

test_cases.test_case: Leave Username blank and submit (text/unspecified required-field representative)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Username field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Leave Password blank and submit (password required-field representative)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with invalid credentials (failed login behavior)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error appears on the form indicating the credentials are invalid; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered username; the form does not redirect to the Dashboard (user remains on the login page)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use Log in when user already has an active authenticated session (precondition violation)
preconditions: User is authenticated (has an active session)
steps: 1. Establish an authenticated session as <existing user> (log in)
2. While still authenticated, navigate to the login page
3. Attempt to click the 'Log in' button or submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: Login action is blocked: the application does not present a functional login form — the user is redirected to the Dashboard (or the login form is not accessible) and no additional login attempt is performed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Access as a guest' while authenticated (precondition violation)
preconditions: User is authenticated (has an active session)
steps: 1. Establish an authenticated session as <existing user> (log in)
2. Navigate to the login page while authenticated
3. Attempt to click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: 'Access as a guest' control is not available for authenticated users (button is not visible or is disabled); clicking it has no effect and the user remains in their authenticated session (no unauthenticated guest browsing is granted)

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Lost password?' link when feature is disabled on this site
preconditions: Login page is displayed
Password recovery feature is disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Observe whether the 'Lost password?' link is present on the login page
2. If the link is present, attempt to click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link is not available or is rendered disabled on this site; clicking it does not navigate to a password-reset flow (no password-reset page is opened)

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username retained after failed login
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter an incorrect password into the Password field
5. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked / error shown: an inline error is displayed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the exact very-long string entered (no silent truncation visible in the field); no redirect to Dashboard occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Unicode/emoji and special characters in Username and Password are retained on failure
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a string containing emoji and non-ASCII Unicode characters into the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter a password containing emoji and special characters into the Password field
5. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked / error shown: an inline error is displayed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the exact Unicode/emoji characters as entered (no character loss or replacement visible in the field); no redirect to Dashboard occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Whitespace-only Username (border of 'empty') results in inline error and retained input
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a Username that consists only of whitespace characters
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter a non-empty password into the Password field
5. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked / error shown: an inline error is displayed (treated as invalid/empty); the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the whitespace characters exactly as entered (no implicit trimming visible); no redirect to Dashboard occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Clicking the disabled "Lost password?" link is blocked on this test site
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session)
The "Lost password?" feature is disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Locate the "Lost password?" link on the login page
2. Click the "Lost password?" link
test_cases.expected_result: Click is blocked / error shown: no navigation or password-reset UI is displayed; the UI remains on the login page and the link does not open a modal or redirect. (Visible state indicates the link is disabled or no action occurs.)

test_cases.test_case: Edit mode toggle reveals Edit-only controls (+ Add a block and Reset page to default)

Dashboard is open
Edit_Mode == false

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle Edit mode on/off repeatedly and verify Edit controls are not stuck visible
preconditions: User is logged in
Dashboard is open
Edit_Mode == false
steps: 1. Click the Edit_Mode checkbox to enable Edit mode
2. Immediately click the Edit_Mode checkbox to disable Edit mode
3. Repeat steps 1 and 2 multiple times in rapid succession (several toggles within 2–3 seconds)
4. After final toggle, ensure Edit_Mode is set to false
test_cases.expected_result: After the rapid toggles complete and Edit_Mode is false, Edit-only controls (+ Add a block, Reset page to default, block Move icons and three-dot menus) are not visible — the UI stabilizes and hiding of edit controls succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Add a block with an extremely long Block_Type_Name in Block Type Chooser

Dashboard is open
Edit_Mode == true
Block_Type_Chooser_Page is reachable via + Add a block
steps: 1. Click the "+ Add a block" button to open the Block_Type_Chooser_Page
2. In the Block_Types repeating group, focus the Block_Type_Name field for a new item
3. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into Block_Type_Name
4. Click the Add button for that repeating group item
test_cases.expected_result: The Add action succeeds; the new block is added to the Dashboard (visible on the Dashboard or in the chooser list). The UI visibly displays the Block_Type_Name (either the full text or a truncated form with an ellipsis) so the long value presence is observable

test_cases.test_case: Add a block then delete it via the block's More menu, then Reset page to default when no custom blocks exist
preconditions: User is logged in
Dashboard is open
Edit_Mode == true
Block_Type_Chooser_Page can add at least one selectable block type
steps: 1. Click the "+ Add a block" button to open the Block_Type_Chooser_Page
2. Click Add on any block type to add it to the Dashboard
3. Return to the Dashboard main view
4. Ensure Edit_Mode == true
5. On the added block, click the More_Menu (three-dot menu)
6. Click the Delete action in the More_Menu
7. Click the "Reset page to default" button
test_cases.expected_result: The Delete action succeeds and the previously added block is removed from the Dashboard; the Reset page to default action then succeeds and the Dashboard layout remains or returns to the default layout (no duplicate or phantom blocks remain)

test_cases.test_case: Timeline empty state displays when Timeline_Block.items_in_range == 0

test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block displays the empty state message "No upcoming activities in the selected range" — the empty-state display succeeds and is visible to the user

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly trigger New event twice to check for duplicate modal instances / double-submit

Dashboard is open
Calendar_Block is visible
steps: 1. Click the "New event" button in the Calendar block
2. Immediately click the "New event" button again before the modal finishes opening or while it is opening
test_cases.expected_result: Only a single new personal event modal instance is presented and no duplicate modals or duplicate event creation workflows are initiated — opening the modal succeeds and no second modal appears

test_cases.test_case: Non-student role is prevented from accessing My Courses
preconditions: Authenticated as a non-student role (e.g., Instructor or Admin without student enrollments)
steps: 1. Sign in as a user with a non-student role
2. Click the My Courses link in the top navigation
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the My Courses page displays an access-denied / insufficient-permissions indicator and does not show the course grid or the page controls (Status_Filter, Search, Sort_By, Layout). Course cards and the card menu actions ('Star this course', 'Remove from view') are not accessible.

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated student with no viewable enrollments is blocked from core course actions
preconditions: Authenticated as a student with zero viewable course enrollments
steps: 1. Sign in as a student account that has no enrolled courses
2. Navigate to the My Courses page
test_cases.expected_result: The My Courses grid is not shown; the page displays an empty-state message indicating there are no enrolled courses and core controls are not usable: Status_Filter, Search, Sort_By and Layout are disabled or hidden, and there are no course cards or card menu actions ('Star this course', 'Remove from view'). No navigation to any Course Page occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Search with a very long query (200+ characters)
preconditions: Authenticated as a student with viewable course enrollments
My Courses page is open
steps: 1. Focus the Search field
2. Enter a query string of 200+ characters into the Search field
3. Press Enter or click the Search control
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds; the Courses Grid updates without UI errors or visual crashes. If no courses match the long query, the Courses Grid shows an empty-state placeholder indicating zero results; the UI remains responsive.

test_cases.test_case: Search with leading and trailing whitespace in the query
preconditions: Authenticated as a student with viewable course enrollments
My Courses page is open
There exists at least one course whose name matches the trimmed form of the intended query
steps: 1. Focus the Search field
2. Enter a query value with leading and trailing whitespace (e.g., '  <search-term>  ')
3. Press Enter or click the Search control
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds; the system treats the query as trimmed (leading/trailing whitespace ignored) and the Courses Grid displays results corresponding to the trimmed query. The Search field shows the trimmed value after execution (no persistent leading/trailing spaces).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive card menu actions: Star then Remove from view
preconditions: Authenticated as a student with viewable course enrollments
My Courses page is open
A target course card is visible in the Courses Grid
steps: 1. Open the three-dot Card_Menu on the target course card
2. Click the 'Star this course' menu action
3. Immediately (without waiting for a full page refresh) open the Card_Menu again for the same target course
4. Click the 'Remove from view' menu action
test_cases.expected_result: Both menu actions succeed; the target course is removed from the current Courses Grid view after 'Remove from view' completes. preconditions: Active authenticated session (logged in as the user whose profile is being viewed)
user must be the profile owner
steps: 1. Navigate to Profile page
2. Click the Edit profile link
3. Expand the General collapsible panel (if collapsed)
4. Enter a name containing special characters and emoji into the First Name field
5. Enter a name containing special characters and emoji into the Last Name field
6. Click Update profile
test_cases.expected_result: Update profile succeeds; saved First Name and Last Name display the exact entered special characters and emoji on the profile detail page (no silent stripping).

test_cases.test_case: Email address with leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: Active authenticated session (logged in as the user whose profile is being viewed)
user must be the profile owner
steps: 1. Navigate to Profile page
2. Click the Edit profile link
3. Expand the General collapsible panel (if collapsed)
4. Enter an otherwise-valid email address with leading and trailing spaces into the Email Address field (e.g. leading and trailing whitespace around the value)
5. Click Update profile
test_cases.expected_result: Update profile succeeds; the saved Email Address shown on the profile detail page has leading and trailing whitespace trimmed (no stored leading/trailing spaces visible).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-submit of Update profile (idempotency)
preconditions: Active authenticated session (logged in as the user whose profile is being viewed)
user must be the profile owner
steps: 1. Navigate to Profile page
2. Click the Edit profile link
3. Make a small change to any editable field (e.g. Last Name)
4. Immediately click Update profile twice in rapid succession (two quick clicks)
5. Observe the UI after the clicks
test_cases.expected_result: Only a single save operation succeeds; the UI shows a single success confirmation and the profile detail reflects one updated value. The second click is ignored or blocked and does not produce a second save or duplicate side-effect.

test_cases.test_case: Upload zero-byte file as New Picture
preconditions: Active authenticated session (logged in as the user whose profile is being viewed)
user must be the profile owner
steps: 1. Navigate to Profile page
2. Click the Edit profile link
3. Expand the User Picture collapsible panel (if collapsed)
4. Upload a zero-byte file to the New Picture upload control
5. Click Update profile
test_cases.expected_result: Update profile is blocked; an inline error is shown next to the New Picture upload field indicating the upload is invalid/empty and no change to the stored profile picture is saved.

test_cases.test_case: Select 'Log out' from user menu redirects to Login page
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
User is currently authenticated (active session) and has access to the top navigation user menu.
steps: 1. Click the <User menu> (user avatar) in the top navigation to open the user menu
2. Click the 'Log out' link in the user menu
test_cases.expected_result: The Login page is displayed with the login form visible; the current authenticated session has been terminated and the user was redirected to the login page.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user navigates directly to the logout endpoint
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Ensure there is no active session (log out or open a new private browser session)
2. In the browser address bar navigate to <logout endpoint URL> (the URL that performs the Log out action)
test_cases.expected_result: Browser is redirected to the <login page URL>; the Login page with the sign-in form is displayed (e.g. Username/Email and Password fields visible). No 'session terminated' success flow occurs and no authenticated session exists after navigation.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated visitor does not see 'Log out' in the user menu
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Ensure there is no active session (log out or open a new private browser session)
2. Navigate to a page that shows the top navigation bar (e.g. Home)
3. Open the top navigation user menu / user avatar dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Log out' / 'Log out' menu item is not visible in the user menu. Instead the user menu shows options to sign in or does not display user-specific menu items. No logout action is available to unauthenticated users.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Log out link
preconditions: User is authenticated and on any protected page
User menu is accessible in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click the user menu in the top navigation
2. Click the 'Log out' link (first click)
3. Immediately click the 'Log out' link again (second click) before the redirect completes
test_cases.expected_result: Logout succeeds: the application processes a single logout and the user is redirected to the login page once; no error message is shown and no duplicate redirects or visible side-effects occur.

test_cases.test_case: Close tab immediately after clicking Log out (server-side logout completion validation)
preconditions: User is authenticated and on any protected page
User menu is accessible in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click the user menu in the top navigation
2. Click the 'Log out' link
3. Immediately close the browser tab before the login page loads
4. Open a new browser tab and navigate to a known protected page URL
test_cases.expected_result: Logout succeeds server-side: access to the protected page is blocked and the new tab is redirected to the login page (no authenticated content is shown).

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back after successful logout to attempt returning to protected page
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
User menu is accessible in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click the user menu in the top navigation
2. Click the 'Log out' link
3. Wait for redirect to the login page to complete
4. Press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation back to protected content is blocked: the browser should display the login page (or re-redirect to it) and no protected content is visible — logout succeeds and protected pages are not accessible from back navigation.

test_cases.test_case: Log out in one tab then perform an authenticated action in another tab that was left open
preconditions: User is authenticated and has two browser tabs open showing protected pages
User menu is accessible in the top navigation on both tabs
steps: 1. In Tab A, click the user menu in the top navigation
2. In Tab A, click the 'Log out' link and wait for redirect to the login page
3. Switch to Tab B (left open, showing a protected page) and click a link or button that requires authentication (for example, navigate to a protected section)
4. Observe the behavior in Tab B
test_cases.expected_result: Authenticated actions in Tab B are blocked after logout: the attempted action is redirected to the login page or otherwise prevented from showing protected content — logout succeeds server-side and other open tabs lose authenticated access.

module_context.summary: Provides the authentication form for users to enter Username and Password, with options for guest access and cookie information, and displays inline errors for invalid credentials.
module_context.where_it_fits: Serves as the primary entry point to the application for unauthenticated users before accessing the Top Navigation (Home, Dashboard, My Courses); successful authentication redirects users to the Dashboard and then allows access to course-level views and the Course Index sidebar.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and at the login page (no active session).

module_context.summary: The Dashboard presents a personalized overview containing a Timeline of upcoming activities and deadlines and a Calendar with month navigation and course filtering.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the Dashboard link, this module is the user's landing/overview page that aggregates information across My Courses and links into course-level views (e.g., Course Page and full calendar). It sits early in the user session lifecycle as the central hub users consult after signing in.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in (authenticated); user has at least one enrolled course or a valid personal calendar.

module_context.summary: Displays all courses a student is enrolled in as visual cards with filtering, searching, sorting, and per-course actions like starring or hiding.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the My Courses link, this page is the student's central catalog of enrolled courses and acts as the entry point to individual Course Pages (which show the course tab bar and breadcrumb navigation). It sits alongside Dashboard and Home as a primary navigation destination for accessing course content.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated as a student with viewable course enrollments

module_context.summary: Displays a course's content organized into collapsible sections with listed activities and resources under each section.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is the main course view reached from the top navigation (My Courses, Dashboard, or Home) and sits below the course title where the horizontal tab bar (Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, Competencies) and Course Index sidebar appear; it is the central place users use to browse course sections and activities during normal course navigation.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and enrolled in the selected course; course exists and contains sections/activities.

module_context.summary: Displays all users enrolled in the course with filtering and alphabetical tools, showing name (linking to profiles), roles, groups, and last access information.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under the course-level horizontal tab bar (Course → Participants) when viewing a course, alongside Course, Grades, Activities, and Competencies; it is the course-scoped view for inspecting enrolled users and their activity/role information.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and viewing a specific course page that has enrolled users, with permission to view the course participants.

module_context.summary: Displays the student's personal grade report for the current course, showing grade items, calculated weights, earned values, percentages, feedback, and the course total aggregation.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the course-level horizontal tab bar (Course > Grades) when viewing a course; it is a course-scoped view tied to the user's enrollment and complements other course tabs like Participants and Activities. The Grades page is reached while navigated to a specific course from the top navigation (Home/Dashboard/My Courses) or the Course Index sidebar.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a student and viewing a specific course where the student is enrolled

module_context.summary: Displays an individual course assignment's open/due dates, full description, submission form options, submission and grading status, and any teacher feedback.
module_context.where_it_fits: This is an activity-level page within a course, reached from the Course Page (via the Course tab or the Course Index sidebar) or via breadcrumbs when viewing an activity. It surfaces assignment-specific submission and grading information and ties into the course's Activities and Grades areas.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged-in user enrolled in the course and viewing the specific assignment activity that is visible to them.

module_context.summary: Provides a consolidated, grouped overview of every course activity with collapsible sections and direct links to each activity page.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from a course's horizontal tab bar under the Activities tab and complements the Course Page and Course Index sidebar by offering an alternative, grouped listing of activities; it appears in the course-level navigation path shown in the breadcrumbs when viewing activities.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and actively viewing a specific course (on the Course Page)

module_context.summary: Displays the user's profile information including contact and privacy details, course links, activity and report summaries, and an editable profile form with collapsible panels for personal data and picture.
module_context.where_it_fits: Reached from the top navigation user menu (User menu → Profile) alongside Grades, Calendar, Private Files and Preferences; it is part of the account area that complements Dashboard/My Courses and course-level views by surfacing personal settings and links to enrolled course profiles and reports.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Active authenticated session (logged in as the user whose profile is being viewed)

module_context.summary: Terminates the current authenticated session and returns the user to the login page.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located in the user menu on the right side of the persistent top navigation bar (accessible from Home, Dashboard, My Courses and course pages), this module is available globally and is used at the end of a user session to end access to protected areas and redirect to the login page.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated (active session) and has access to the top navigation user menu.
